MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
E. Jacob Lubarsky, Esq. of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4127), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party David Astle

RULING:
Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary / judgment / adjudication. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for summary adjudications as to issue 2 (strict liability) is granted. A contractor defendant who installs defective products is not strictly liable for injuries caused by the products. (Monte Vista Development Corp. v. Superior Court (1991) 226 Cal.App.3d 1681.) Plaintiff's response to Defendant's interrogatory seeking all facts that Defendant is strictly liable contains no information that Defendant ever sold, manufactured or supplied any asbestos containing product. Plaintiff's evidence in opposition does not create a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied. The remaining issues raised by Defendant's motion are moot. Defendant has withdrawn its motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication on issues 1 (negligence) and 4 (premises owner/contractor liability). Plaintiff has dismissed the causes of action that are the subjects of issues 3 (false representation) and 5 (punitive damages). Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077
